pipeline.c(3) Library Functions Manual pipeline.c(3)

NAME

pipeline.c - Three-thread render pipeline implementation.

SYNOPSIS

Data Structures


struct pipeline_frame_t
struct frame_queue_s
struct session_pipeline_s

Typedefs


typedef struct frame_queue_s frame_queue_t

Functions


asciichat_error_t session_pipeline_create (session_capture_ctx_t *capture, session_display_ctx_t *display, session_pipeline_t **out)
asciichat_error_t session_pipeline_run_main (session_pipeline_t *pipeline, session_should_exit_fn should_exit, session_keyboard_handler_fn keyboard_handler, void *user_data)

Variables


bool g_snapshot_first_frame_rendered

Detailed Description

Three-thread render pipeline implementation.

Definition in file pipeline.c.

Typedef Documentation

typedef struct frame_queue_s frame_queue_t

Function Documentation

asciichat_error_t session_pipeline_create (session_capture_ctx_t * capture, session_display_ctx_t * display, session_pipeline_t ** out)

Create and start the capture thread and (if --render-file) encode thread.

Parameters

capture Capture context (must have real media_source_t for synchronous mode)
display Display context (may have render_file set for --render-file mode)
out Pointer to receive allocated pipeline context

Returns

ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error code otherwise

Note

The pipeline does NOT own capture or display — caller must keep them alive until session_pipeline_destroy() returns.

Definition at line 382 of file pipeline.c.

386 {
387     if (!capture || !display || !out) {
388         return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "session_pipeline_create: NULL argument");
389     }
390 
391     session_pipeline_t *p = SAFE_CALLOC(1, sizeof(*p), session_pipeline_t *);
392     p->capture = capture;
393     p->display = display;
394     p->display_queue = frame_queue_create(4, "display");
395     p->encode_queue = frame_queue_create(256, "encode");  // Large buffer for slow encoding
396 
397     // Check if display has render_file configured (will be checked in encode thread)
398     // We store a flag to know whether to enqueue frames for encoding
399     double render_fps = session_display_get_render_fps(display);
400     bool has_file = session_display_has_render_file(display);
401 
402     // Enable encode thread if render_file is available
403     // Note: has_file can be true even if render_file creation failed (ctx->render_file exists)
404     // OR if render_fps is explicitly set for playback speed control
405     p->has_render_file = render_fps > 0 || has_file;
406     log_info("[PIPELINE_CREATE] render_fps=%.1f, has_file=%d, has_render_file=%d",
407              render_fps, has_file ? 1 : 0, p->has_render_file ? 1 : 0);
408 
409     atomic_store_bool(&p->stop, false);
410     atomic_store_u64(&p->first_frame_ns, 0);
411 
412     // Start capture thread
413     if (asciichat_thread_create(&p->capture_tid, "pipeline_capture", pipeline_capture_thread, p) != 0) {
414         frame_queue_destroy(p->display_queue);
415         frame_queue_destroy(p->encode_queue);
416         SAFE_FREE(p);
417         return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INIT, "session_pipeline_create: failed to start capture thread");
418     }
419 
420     // Start encode thread only if render_file is active
421     if (p->has_render_file) {
422         if (asciichat_thread_create(&p->encode_tid, "pipeline_encode", pipeline_encode_thread, p) != 0) {
423             atomic_store_bool(&p->stop, true);
424             asciichat_thread_join(&p->capture_tid, NULL);
425             frame_queue_destroy(p->display_queue);
426             frame_queue_destroy(p->encode_queue);
427             SAFE_FREE(p);
428             return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INIT, "session_pipeline_create: failed to start encode thread");
429         }
430     }
431 
432     *out = p;
433     return ASCIICHAT_OK;
434 }

    asciichat_thread_join(), atomic_store_bool(),
    atomic_store_u64(), session_pipeline_s::capture,
    session_pipeline_s::capture_tid, session_pipeline_s::display,
    session_pipeline_s::display_queue,
    session_pipeline_s::encode_queue,
    session_pipeline_s::encode_tid, ERROR_INIT,
    ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, session_pipeline_s::first_frame_ns,
    session_pipeline_s::has_render_file, log_info,
    SAFE_CALLOC, SAFE_FREE,
    session_display_get_render_fps(),
    session_display_has_render_file(), SET_ERRNO, and
    session_pipeline_s::stop.


Referenced by session_render_loop().






asciichat_error_t session_pipeline_destroy
  (session_pipeline_t * pipeline)


Stop threads and free pipeline.




Waits for capture and encode threads to exit cleanly.




Parameters


pipeline Pipeline context (safe to call with
  NULL)


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK


Definition at line 538 of file pipeline.c.


538                                                                          {
539     if (!pipeline) return ASCIICHAT_OK;
540 
541     log_info("[PIPELINE] Destroying pipeline, waiting for threads...");
542 
543     // Signal threads to stop (may already be stopped)
544     atomic_store_bool(&pipeline->stop, true);
545 
546     // Wake up any threads blocked on condition variable waits
547     // so they can check the stop flag and exit immediately
548     // instead of waiting for timeouts (e.g., 100ms frame_queue_pop timeout)
549     if (pipeline->display_queue) {
550         mutex_lock(&pipeline->display_queue->mu);
551         cond_broadcast(&pipeline->display_queue->not_empty);
552         cond_broadcast(&pipeline->display_queue->not_full);
553         mutex_unlock(&pipeline->display_queue->mu);
554     }
555     if (pipeline->encode_queue) {
556         mutex_lock(&pipeline->encode_queue->mu);
557         cond_broadcast(&pipeline->encode_queue->not_empty);
558         cond_broadcast(&pipeline->encode_queue->not_full);
559         mutex_unlock(&pipeline->encode_queue->mu);
560     }
561 
562     // Drain and wait for capture thread
563     if (asciichat_thread_is_initialized(&pipeline->capture_tid)) {
564         asciichat_thread_join(&pipeline->capture_tid, NULL);
565     }
566 
567     // Drain and wait for encode thread
568     if (asciichat_thread_is_initialized(&pipeline->encode_tid)) {
569         asciichat_thread_join(&pipeline->encode_tid, NULL);
570     }
571 
572     // Flush queues to free any remaining frames
573     frame_queue_flush(pipeline->display_queue, free_frame_generic);
574     frame_queue_flush(pipeline->encode_queue, free_frame_generic);
575 
576     // Don't free queue structures - debug_sync monitoring thread may still be accessing
577     // the condition variables. Queues will be cleaned up with the pipeline structure.
578     SAFE_FREE(pipeline);
579 
580     log_info("[PIPELINE] Pipeline destroyed");
581     return ASCIICHAT_OK;
582 }






References ASCIICHAT_OK,
    asciichat_thread_is_initialized(), asciichat_thread_join(),
    atomic_store_bool(), session_pipeline_s::capture_tid,
    cond_broadcast(), session_pipeline_s::display_queue,
    session_pipeline_s::encode_queue,
    session_pipeline_s::encode_tid, log_info,
    frame_queue_s::mu, mutex_lock, mutex_unlock,
    frame_queue_s::not_empty, frame_queue_s::not_full,
    SAFE_FREE, and session_pipeline_s::stop.


Referenced by session_render_loop().






asciichat_error_t session_pipeline_run_main
  (session_pipeline_t * pipeline, session_should_exit_fn
  should_exit, session_keyboard_handler_fn keyboard_handler, void *
  user_data)


Main thread loop: drives terminal output and keyboard
  handling.




Pops frames from display queue, converts to ASCII, writes to
    terminal. Handles snapshot delay timer and exit conditions.




Parameters


pipeline Pipeline context created by
  session_pipeline_create()


should_exit Callback to check if rendering should
  stop


keyboard_handler Optional keyboard handler callback


user_data Opaque data passed to callbacks


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success


Note


This function blocks until should_exit() returns
  true or capture ends. Signals capture/encode threads to stop before
  returning.


Definition at line 436 of file pipeline.c.


441 {
442     if (!pipeline || !should_exit) {
443         return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "session_pipeline_run_main: NULL argument");
444     }
445 
446     log_info("[PIPELINE_MAIN] Starting main thread loop");
447 
448     bool snapshot_mode = GET_OPTION(snapshot_mode);
449     bool snapshot_done = false;
450 
451     while (!should_exit(user_data) && !atomic_load_bool(&pipeline->stop) && !snapshot_done) {
452         pipeline_frame_t *frame = (pipeline_frame_t *)frame_queue_pop(pipeline->display_queue, 100 * NS_PER_MS_INT);
453 
454         if (!frame) continue;  // timeout, check should_exit again
455 
456         // Validate frame structure exists before accessing fields
457         if (!frame_is_valid(frame)) {
458             log_error("Received invalid frame: corrupted or NULL");
459             free_frame(frame);
460             frame = NULL;
461             continue;
462         }
463 
464         // Check for EOF sentinel (NULL pixels)
465         if (!frame->pixels) {
466             // EOF sentinel
467             log_info("[PIPELINE_MAIN_EOF] Received EOF sentinel, stopping");
468             free_frame(frame);
469             frame = NULL;
470             break;
471         }
472 
473         // Log frame details after validation
474         log_debug("[PIPELINE_MAIN_POP] frame=%p, pixels=%p, w=%d, h=%d",
475                   (void *)frame, (void *)frame->pixels, frame->w, frame->h);
476 
477         // Check if help screen is active - if so, don't render ASCII frames
478         bool help_is_active = pipeline->display && keyboard_help_is_active(pipeline->display);
479 
480         if (!help_is_active) {
481             log_info("[PIPELINE_MAIN_RENDER] Converting frame to ASCII: %dx%d", frame->w, frame->h);
482             // Convert to ASCII
483             image_t tmp = { .w = frame->w, .h = frame->h, .pixels = (rgb_pixel_t *)frame->pixels };
484             char *ascii = session_display_convert_to_ascii(pipeline->display, &tmp);
485             free_frame(frame);
486             frame = NULL;
487 
488             if (ascii) {
489                 // Write ASCII to terminal
490                 session_display_write_ascii(pipeline->display, ascii);
491                 SAFE_FREE(ascii);
492             }
493         } else {
494             free_frame(frame);
495             frame = NULL;
496         }
497 
498         // Keyboard polling
499         if (keyboard_handler) {
500             keyboard_key_t key = keyboard_read_nonblocking();
501             if (key != KEY_NONE) {
502                 log_debug("PIPELINE_KEYBOARD: Received key=%d", key);
503                 keyboard_handler(NULL, (int)key, user_data);  // NULL capture ctx
504             }
505         }
506 
507         // Snapshot mode: check if elapsed time has reached snapshot_delay duration
508         // This check runs every iteration after first frame is rendered (when display.c sets g_snapshot_first_frame_rendered)
509         if (snapshot_mode && !snapshot_done) {
510             if (g_snapshot_first_frame_rendered && g_snapshot_first_frame_rendered_ns > 0) {
511                 double snapshot_delay = GET_OPTION(snapshot_delay);
512                 uint64_t now_ns = time_get_ns();
513                 uint64_t elapsed_ns = now_ns - g_snapshot_first_frame_rendered_ns;
514                 double elapsed_sec = (double)elapsed_ns / (double)NS_PER_SEC_INT;
515 
516                 if ((unsigned long)(elapsed_sec * 10) % 10 == 0 || elapsed_sec < 0.2) {
517                     log_info("[SNAPSHOT_PIPELINE] CHECK: elapsed=%.3f target=%.2f", elapsed_sec, snapshot_delay);
518                 }
519 
520                 // snapshot_delay=0 means exit after first frame
521                 // snapshot_delay>0 means wait that many seconds before exiting
522                 bool should_snapshot_exit = (snapshot_delay == 0.0) || (elapsed_sec >= snapshot_delay);
523 
524                 if (should_snapshot_exit) {
525                     log_info("[SNAPSHOT_PIPELINE] EXITING - elapsed=%.3f target=%.2f", elapsed_sec, snapshot_delay);
526                     snapshot_done = true;
527                 }
528             }
529         }
530     }
531 
532     log_info("[PIPELINE_MAIN] Main loop exiting, signaling threads to stop");
533     atomic_store_bool(&pipeline->stop, true);
534 
535     return ASCIICHAT_OK;
536 }






References ASCIICHAT_OK, atomic_load_bool(),
    atomic_store_bool(), session_pipeline_s::display,
    session_pipeline_s::display_queue, ERROR_INVALID_PARAM,
    frame, g_snapshot_first_frame_rendered,
    g_snapshot_first_frame_rendered_ns, GET_OPTION,
    keyboard_help_is_active(), keyboard_read_nonblocking(),
    log_debug, log_error, log_info, NS_PER_MS_INT,
    NS_PER_SEC_INT, SAFE_FREE,
    session_display_convert_to_ascii(),
    session_display_write_ascii(), SET_ERRNO,
    should_exit(), session_pipeline_s::stop, and
    time_get_ns().
